title: Achneria mucronata
taxonomy: Achneria mucronata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. This plant is native to South America. The family Anacardiaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the sumac or cashew family. Members of this family are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Achneria mucronata is a part of the genus Achneria, which is a monotypic genus meaning it contains only one species.

Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The leaves of Achneria mucronata are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation.
